speaker: THALIARD
text: So this is Tyre, and this the court. Here

speaker: THALIARD
text: must I kill King Pericles; and if I do it not, I am

speaker: THALIARD
text: sure to be hanged at home. ’Tis dangerous. Well, I

speaker: THALIARD
text: perceive he was a wise fellow and had good discretion

speaker: THALIARD
text: that, being bid to ask what he would of the

speaker: THALIARD
text: king, desired he might know none of his secrets.

speaker: THALIARD
text: Now do I see he had some reason for ’t, for if a

speaker: THALIARD
text: king bid a man be a villain, he’s bound by the

speaker: THALIARD
text: indenture of his oath to be one. Husht! Here

speaker: THALIARD
text: comes the lords of Tyre.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: You shall not need, my fellow peers of Tyre,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Further to question me of your king’s departure.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: His sealed commission left in trust with me

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Does speak sufficiently he’s gone to travel.

speaker: THALIARD
text: How? The King gone?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: If further yet you will be satisfied

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Why, as it were, unlicensed of your loves

speaker: HELICANUS
text: He would depart, I’ll give some light unto you.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Being at Antioch—

speaker: THALIARD
text: What from Antioch?

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Royal Antiochus, on what cause I know not,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Took some displeasure at him—at least he judged so;

speaker: HELICANUS
text: And doubting lest he had erred or sinned,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: To show his sorrow, he’d correct himself;

speaker: HELICANUS
text: So puts himself unto the shipman’s toil,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: With whom each minute threatens life or death.

speaker: THALIARD
text: Well, I perceive I shall not be hanged

speaker: THALIARD
text: now, although I would; but since he’s gone, the

speaker: THALIARD
text: King’s ears it must please. He ’scaped the land to

speaker: THALIARD
text: perish at the sea. I’ll present myself.—Peace to the

speaker: THALIARD
text: lords of Tyre!

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Lord Thaliard from Antiochus is welcome.

speaker: THALIARD
text: From him I come with message unto princely

speaker: THALIARD
text: Pericles, but since my landing I have understood

speaker: THALIARD
text: your lord has betook himself to unknown travels.

speaker: THALIARD
text: Now message must return from whence it came.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: We have no reason to desire it,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Commended to our master, not to us.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Yet ere you shall depart, this we desire:

speaker: HELICANUS
text: As friends to Antioch, we may feast in Tyre.
